items.title: Tech titans team up to form optical interconnect alliance to solve the AI buildout's big data bottleneck — Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom & more set sights on building PHY to break through the limitations of copper
items.description: AMD, Nvidia, Microsoft, Broadcom, and Meta have formed an Optical Compute Interconnect Multi-Source Agreement (OCI MSA) to develop a standardized optical interconnect for AI data centers, with the aspiration to build a PHY capable of handling speeds of up to 3.2 Tb/s.

Subscribe to our newsletter This week, AMD, Broadcom, Nvidia, OpenAI, Meta, and Microsoft announced plans to standardize a protocol-agnostic, scale-up interconnect for AI data centers. The Optical Compute Interconnect Multi-Source Agreement (OCI MSA) group is tasked with defining an open connectivity specification for optical interconnections in AI data centers. This would allow for higher domain scale-up sizes, and enable a multi-vendor supply chain for optical interconnects, which the ongoing AI infrastructure buildout demands.The group's primary goal is to enable data centers to scale by using optical interconnections rather than relying solely on copper, which is currently hitting its physical limits for optimal data transfer speeds and power consumption. Copper is also facing significant supply chain constraints, and an industry-wide shift to optical interconnections would alleviate some of this demand. An optical interconnection would also bolster data transfer speeds, crucial for large-scale AI workloads.For copper, pushing electrical signals to high speeds results in signal degradation and unsustainable levels of power consumption; the solution is optical interconnects. Copper is inherently a lossy, resistant medium, necessitating huge amounts of power to send data over distances at high speeds. An optical physical layer (PHY) can overcome this electrical resistance challenge, allowing for higher-speed data transmission. The goal of the newly-founded group is to develop a PHY capable of delivering up to 3.2Tb/s and beyond.Article continues below You may like AMD, Broadcom, and Nvidia join hyperscalers to define optical scale-up interconnect of the future for AI clusters Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneck Start-up plans to use terahertz radio frequencies for communication between servers instead of copper or optical connections Given that power is an ongoing challenge in AI data center buildouts, a solution that not only stabilizes power usage but also increases interconnection speeds seems like an obvious choice.Optical cables would let more systems be concurrently connected over greater distances, without many of the penalties that come with copper, improving scale-up domains. However, optics also comes with its own downsides: Failure rates, increased heat output, higher overall costs, and overall failure rates. With the technology nascent in its application, new standards must be created in order for it to mature.Going platform-agnostic (Image credit: Intel)"Optical cables and the silicon photonics technology already exist when it comes to connecting different switches as part of a pluggable transfer ecosystem.” said Vivek Raghunathan, CEO and co-founder at Xscape Photonics, in a recent interview with Tom's Hardware Premium. Indeed, TSMC's COUPE technology is a foundational bedrock for enabling optical and photonics in chips; where the new OCI MSA standard comes in is to enable these physical chips to effectively travel across the same lanes.The open standard developed by the OCI MSA would allow multiple vendors within the optical supply chain to offer components to a singlular, unified spec. In theory, it should drive down the cost of optics and optical interconnects at scale. Moreover, it divests the sole reliance on TSMC, as the standard would ensure interoperability between products and chips made with COUPE, and those using alternative CPO packaging platforms, effectively de-risking optical supply chains in the process.If co-packaged optics were introduced into data centers without an effective rulebook or guidelines to govern them, each vendor design would become proprietary, siloed between one another. So for larger-scale data centers that run multiple ecosystems, the OCI MSA's new spec defines a standard to let them drive on the same roads and use the same data pathways. This isn't about hooking together systems and getting them to understand each other via NVLink or UALink, but instead about offering a physical foundation to allow those protocols to travel over fibre connections.Within a data center, the OCI MSA's newly defined standard would allow a data center to theoretically run NVLink for Nvidia chips and UALink for AMD hardware, while making use of the same underlying optical infrastructure, at higher speeds than copper can realistically allow.“Fundamentally, the current copper-based interconnects just cannot meet that bandwidth requirement," Raghunathan continued. The OCI MSA's standard supports a range of optical solutions, such as pluggable optical modules, on-board optics, and chips using co-packaged optics. This would effectively break the barriers that copper currently employs. What to read next Nvidia invests $4 billion into photonics firms in a bid to bolster data center interconnect supply chains UALink roadmap plots course to optimized AI data center interconnects Bill Gates-backed silicon photonics startup develops optical transistors 10,000x smaller than current tech Getting in on the ground (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The companies involved in the newly formed alliance are all of the usual suspects: Nvidia, AMD, Microsoft, OpenAI, Broadcom, and Meta all create their own AI accelerators in some way, shape or form. Letting them assist in defining the new PHY means their products will be supported by it from day one."Scale up focused optical technologies, protocols, and switch architectures are foundational to building scalable, multi rack, high performance AI compute domains. The OCI MSA advances this vision with a forward-looking physical layer specification setting the stage for open standards, differentiated implementations and systems architecture innovation.” said Saurabh Dighe, Corporate VP of Azure and Architecture at Microsoft.From an early deployment at 200Gbps speeds all the way up to the 3.2Tb/s outlined by the alliance, it's becoming increasingly clear that to serve larger frontier models, or build toward vastly more complex AI data centers, optics is no longer an exploratory zone. It must be rolled out and served directly, if hyperscalers and AI model developers have their way."By equipping best-in-class compute with state-of-the-art optics, the OCI MSA can deliver the scale and performance required by the next era of super-intelligence." says Gilad Shainer, SVP of networking at Nvidia. With superintelligence on the mind, speed is a non-negotiable, and the standards being drawn up reflect the rapid advancement of data center technology.
items.author: Sayem Ahmed
items.titleZh: 科技巨头联手组建光学互连联盟，破解AI基建的大数据瓶颈——英伟达、AMD和博通等公司着力开发物理层技术以突破铜缆限制。
items.descriptionZh:
本周，AMD、博通、英伟达、OpenAI、Meta和微软共同宣布成立光学计算互连多源协议组织，旨在为人工智能数据中心定义一个协议无关的、可扩展的光互连开放标准。这一举措旨在应对当前AI基础设施扩建中铜互连所面临的物理极限、功耗挑战和供应链约束，通过推动光学互连的标准化，构建一个多供应商的开放生态系统，从而支持更大规模、更高性能的AI计算集群。

当前数据中心主要依赖铜缆进行设备间互连，但在追求极高数据传输速率时，电信号在铜介质中会产生严重的衰减和失真，导致功耗急剧上升且传输距离受限。光学互连利用光子而非电子传输数据，其物理层能从根本上克服电阻损耗，实现更高速、更长距离且能效更高的数据传输。新成立的OCI MSA组织的核心目标是制定一个物理层规范，支持高达3.2Tb/s及以上的传输速率，为AI数据中心的横向扩展提供基础。

该标准的关键创新点在于其“协议无关”和“供应商中立”的特性。它并非定义新的通信协议，而是为现有的互连协议提供一个统一的光学物理层基础。这意味着数据中心可以在同一套光互连基础设施上，同时运行英伟达的NVLink、AMD的UALink或其他专有互连协议，从而实现异构硬件环境的高效整合。此外，标准将涵盖可插拔光模块、板载光学以及共封装光学等多种形态，提供灵活的部署选项。

在性能方面，光学互连有望突破铜缆在带宽和能效上的天花板。铜缆在高速长距离传输时面临严峻挑战，而光学方案能够稳定支持从200Gbps直至3.2Tb/s的速率，同时显著降低单位比特的传输功耗。这对于训练万亿参数级别的前沿AI模型至关重要，因为此类任务需要海量计算节点间持续进行低延迟、高带宽的数据交换。

此举对产业链将产生深远影响。首先，一个开放的标准化接口将打破当前光学供应链可能存在的技术壁垒和供应商锁定，鼓励更多厂商参与竞争，从而有望降低光学互连的整体成本。其次，它降低了对单一制造技术的依赖。虽然台积电的COUPE等硅光子集成技术是重要的使能基础，但该标准将确保采用不同共封装光学平台的产品之间能够互操作，增强了供应链的弹性。

从应用场景看，该标准直接服务于构建大规模、多机柜的AI计算域。随着AI模型规模不断膨胀，计算集群需要跨越数百甚至数千个加速器，节点间的互连带宽和延迟成为系统性能的关键瓶颈。光学互连是实现这种“横向扩展”的理想选择，它能以更低的功耗连接更多系统，覆盖更远距离，从而支持未来更复杂的超级智能计算基础设施。

各大科技巨头联合推动此事，反映了行业共识：光学互连已从探索性技术转变为AI数据中心发展的必然路径。微软Azure架构副总裁指出，面向扩展的光学技术、协议和交换架构是构建可扩展高性能AI计算域的基础。英伟达高管也强调，为顶尖计算配备尖端光学技术是实现下一代超智能所需规模与性能的关键。随着标准的制定与落地，光学互连将为核心AI基础设施建设开启新的篇章。

items.title: Nvidia claims 1 million times better path tracing performance is coming in future gaming GPUs — says current GPUs are already 10,000x faster than Pascal
items.description: At GDC 2026, Nvidia held a presentation somehow aimed at gamers and not data center clients. Still, it was sprinkled with AI pat-on-the-backs, with the company touting that its future gaming GPUs will offer 1,000,000 better path tracing performance. And the current-gen Blackwell family is apparently already 100,000x better due to dedicated Tensor and RT cores.

You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Despite increasing competition from Intel and AMD, Nvidia's RTX lineup remains the best hardware for ray tracing and path tracing in games. Ever since Turing, RTX 20 series, the company has made significant strides — mostly leveraging AI and neural rendering — to increase graphical fidelity without compromising performance. Now, at GDC 2026, it's claiming that the future holds an even more impressive milestone. Driving Innovation and RTX Advances with John Spitzer, VP of Developer and Performance Technology - YouTube Watch On During the presentation, John Spitzer (Dev & Performance VP) presented a line graph that plotted the progress of ray tracing and path tracing performance in Nvidia's gaming GPUs. At the far-left corner, we see Pascal, aka the legendary RTX 10 series, which came out a decade ago. Comparing that to today's Blackwell GPUs (RTX 50), the path tracing performance has apparently improved by 10,000 times already.That's largely due to a focus on hardware-accelerated neural rendering enabled by dedicated RT and Tensor cores that handle machine learning inside Nvidia GPUs. Features like DLSS are entirely reliant on AI; the ability to piece together frame data more accurately in both upscaling and frame-gen situations is only possible due to models trained on Nvidia's supercomputers.Article continues below You may like Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says "the future is neural rendering" at CES 2026 Moore Threads unveils next-gen gaming GPU with 15x performance and 50x ray tracing improvement Nvidia introduces DLSS 4.5 and Multi Frame Generation 6X at CES 2026 Spitzer says that Moore's Law is dead and that silicon advancements alone wouldn't be enough to generate photorealistic visuals in his lifetime. Nvidia wants to achieve a level of graphical fidelity that's indistinguishable from real life, but that would require a "hundred or thousand times more computational power" — this is where AI becomes the catalyst. (Image credit: Nvidia)In the future, AI advances will take gaming GPUs to 1,000,000 times better path tracing performance when compared to the RTX 10 series. Newer, faster, more efficient hardware blocks will basically make neural rendering the default going forward, as already claimed by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang. Games would "look like a film" while still running smoothly due to multiple frames being interpolated in real-time by AI.None of this is a revelation — of course, things are supposed to get better over time — but the wait might not be too long. The next-gen Rubin GPUs from Nvidia, slated to launch sometime between 2027 and 2028, could usher in this 1-million-times better path tracing reality. The list of games supporting path tracing is already growing at a rapid pace, with Resident Evil Requiem being the latest addition.As such, the presentation also included some bits about new path tracing technologies, such as ReSTIR (recent spatiotemporal resampling algorithms) and RTX Mega Geometry. To showcase this, Nvidia brought a tech demo for Witcher 4 with over two trillion triangles in the scene, depicting realistic foliage and lighting simultaneously. Make sure to check out the video linked above for more details.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 英伟达声称未来游戏显卡光追性能将提升百万倍——表示现有显卡已比帕斯卡架构快万倍
items.descriptionZh:
在2026年游戏开发者大会（GDC）上，英伟达开发者与性能技术副总裁约翰·斯皮策展示了公司GPU在光线追踪与路径追踪性能方面的巨大飞跃。尽管面临英特尔和AMD的竞争加剧，英伟达的RTX系列目前仍是游戏光线追踪和路径追踪的最佳硬件选择。

自图灵架构（RTX 20系列）以来，英伟达主要借助人工智能和神经渲染技术，在不牺牲性能的前提下显著提升了图形保真度。斯皮策展示的性能进展曲线图显示，与十年前的帕斯卡架构（RTX 10系列）相比，当前布莱克架构（RTX 50系列）的路径追踪性能已提升约**1万倍**。这一成就主要归功于专注于硬件加速的神经渲染，该技术由英伟达GPU中专用的RT核心和Tensor核心驱动，用于处理机器学习任务。例如，DLSS（深度学习超级采样）完全依赖于AI，其能够在超分辨率和帧生成场景中更精确地重构帧数据，正是基于英伟达超级计算机训练的模型。

斯皮策指出，摩尔定律已失效，仅靠硅基工艺进步不足以在其有生之年实现照片级真实感的视觉效果。英伟达的目标是实现与现实难以区分的图形保真度，但这需要“数百或数千倍的计算能力”，而AI正是实现这一目标的催化剂。英伟达CEO黄仁勋也曾表示“未来是神经渲染”。未来，AI的进步将使游戏GPU的路径追踪性能达到RTX 10系列的**100万倍**。更新、更快、更高效的硬件模块将使神经渲染成为未来的默认技术。由于AI实时插值生成多帧，游戏将能够“看起来像电影”同时保持流畅运行。

这一未来可能并不遥远。英伟达下一代鲁宾架构GPU预计在2027年至2028年间推出，可能将带来这百万倍性能提升的现实。目前支持路径追踪的游戏列表正在迅速增长，最新加入的是《生化危机：安魂曲》。

演示中还介绍了一些新的路径追踪技术，如ReSTIR（近期时空重采样算法）和RTX Mega Geometry。为展示后者，英伟达带来了《巫师4》的技术演示，场景中包含超过**两万亿个三角形**，同时描绘了逼真的植被和光照效果。这一进展标志着，通过硬件与AI的深度融合，实时渲染正在向影视级视觉体验迈进。

items.title: The ongoing Strait of Hormuz blockage will impact the semiconductor and AI industries with Aluminum, Helium and LNG shortages — and with no timeline for re-opening, supply chains face significant challenges
items.description: As global oil prices spike due to the ongoing pseudo-blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, other shortages are looming, and they could hit the chip and AI industries dramatically. Aluminum and helium for chip production have been impacted, but shortages of LNG could also cause problems for gas turbines powering many AI data centers.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter With the Strait of Hormuz effectively blockaded as part of the ongoing U.S.-Iran war, a number of shortages beyond gasoline are brewing: Helium, aluminum, and liquid natural gas (LNG) are all in increasingly short supply. This bottleneck could hit a wide range of industries, including chip manufacturing and data centers, as they are key components of day-to-day operation.And with Iran now confirmed to have released sea mines into the Strait, its closure looks set to continue for a few more weeks, posing a grave threat to many global industries and the global economy in turn.More than just oilThe first and most immediate impact of Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz was that oil prices went parabolic. With around 25% of the global shipbound-oil trade passing through the Strait, cutting it off from the global economy sent everyone scurrying for other sources, raising prices.Article continues below You may like Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock Shortages of crucial chip packaging material threatens AI accelerator supply chains Glass cloth could be the next great AI shortage, as major manufacturers scramble to secure critical material But the Strait isn't just a transit route for oil tankers; some 11% of global seaborne trade by volume passes through it each year. Ships that transit the waterway are responsible for 20% of the global LNG market - the same gas that powers much of the world's energy infrastructure.The Middle East is one of the world's largest exporters of refined aluminum, importing the unwrought material before processing and shipping it out. The Middle East is responsible for around 9% of global aluminum smelting capacity, states Reuters. And some regional suppliers have announced incoming shortages. Others have simply shut down refineries while the conflict is ongoing, but they will be hard to start back up again.Copper is also impacted, partly because it flowed through the Strait and partly because Iran was a major producer. Copper was already in short supply because of explosive demand following the AI data center buildout announcements made in 2025.The helium supply has been impacted by the war as well. Drone strikes knocked out QatarEnergy's Ras Laffan complex over a week ago, and it still hasn't come back online. Sherwood reports that Qatar alone is responsible for around 30% of global helium production, as a byproduct of its LNG production. With that supply no longer getting out, producers are shutting down their wells, which in turn cuts out helium supply lines.And to top it all off, even shipping containers are in short supply. As ships get stuck waiting at the Strait for a chance to transit, they're not reaching their destinations and unloading their containers. The just-in-time nature of the global shipping industry means those containers now aren't available to ship something else back the other way, causing further disruption.Why these materials matter (Image credit: Intel)For some of the supply shortages caused by the closing of the Strait of Hormuz, the reason for concern is obvious. Oil is needed for petro-chemicals, so a shortage drives up prices at the fuel pump, but also of just about everything else. One third of China's total LNG use comes from the Strait, according to the Oxford Institute for Energy Studies.Although some materials are a little harder to quantify, their impact is no less dramatic. And they could have an outsized impact on the chip and AI data center industries. What to read next Brace for a barren landscape of new hardware launches, as AI demand reshapes the world of consumer electronics How China’s control of battery supply chains is becoming a critical risk for U.S. military power and AI initiatives A deeper look at the tightened chipmaking supply chain, and where it may be headed in 2026 LNG is used to produce energy via gas turbines, and is of major import for powering industries in Asia, including China and Taiwan. But it's also been used heavily to run new AI data centers such as those run by xAI and OpenAI. CNBC reports that European LNG prices have already risen by over 60%. As prices for gas rise, energy prices will, in turn, and that will make running these AI data centers more expensive.Helium is incredibly important for chip production. As a chemically inert gas, Helium is used as a blanket and purge gas when producing silicon wafers. Its high thermal conductivity makes it a useful coolant when liquified, too. It's important for manufacturing the chips, as well. If the shortage lasts more than two weeks, then it could pose significant challenges.South Korean firms like Samsung and SK hynix are said to be closely monitoring the situation, as any long-term disruption could impact the supply of memory. That's about the last thing the already-constrained industry needs. TSMC in Taiwan could also be impacted, although some Taiwanese companies claim to hold years worth of Helium in reserve.Closed for the forseeableWith no clear way to open the strait, no timeline for when efforts to do so might even start, and no suggestion that any of the parties involved want to even begin the process, the Strait looks set to be closed for the foreseeable future. That means global trade disruption will worsen, and associated supply chain problems will mount.If you have been holding out to upgrade something when prices for memory or anything else besides might return to normal, you might be waiting a very long time.
items.author: Jon Martindale
items.titleZh: 霍尔木兹海峡持续封锁将导致铝、氦气和液化天然气短缺，影响半导体和人工智能行业，且海峡暂无重启时间表，供应链面临严峻挑战
items.descriptionZh:
霍尔木兹海峡因持续的美伊冲突实际上已被封锁，引发的短缺问题远超汽油范畴：氦气、铝材和液化天然气（LNG）的供应正日益紧张。这一瓶颈可能冲击包括芯片制造和数据中心在内的众多产业，因为这些材料是其日常运营的关键组成部分。随着伊朗被证实已在海峡布设水雷，预计封锁将持续数周，进而对许多全球产业及世界经济构成严重威胁。

**影响远超石油**
伊朗封锁霍尔木兹海峡最直接、最即时的影响是油价飙升。全球约25%的海运石油贸易需经由此海峡，切断这条通道迫使各方仓促寻找替代来源，从而推高了价格。然而，霍尔木兹海峡不仅是油轮的通道，每年全球约11%的海运贸易量需经过此处。通过该水路的船舶承担了全球20%的LNG运输，而正是这种天然气为全球大量能源基础设施提供动力。

中东是全球最大的精炼铝出口地之一，其进口铝坯进行加工后再出口。据路透社报道，中东约占全球铝冶炼产能的9%。一些地区供应商已宣布即将面临短缺，另一些则在冲突期间直接关闭了精炼厂，而这些工厂重启将非常困难。

铜的供应也受到影响，部分因其运输需经过海峡，部分因伊朗本身是主要生产国。铜供应本就因2025年AI数据中心建设热潮引发的爆炸性需求而紧张。

氦气供应同样受到战争冲击。无人机袭击在一周多前摧毁了卡塔尔能源公司的拉斯拉凡综合设施，该设施至今尚未恢复运行。Sherwood报告称，仅卡塔尔就占全球氦气产量的约30%，这些氦气是其LNG生产的副产品。随着供应中断，生产商正在关闭气井，进而切断了氦气供应链。

雪上加霜的是，连航运集装箱也出现短缺。由于船只被困在海峡等待通行机会，它们无法抵达目的地并卸下集装箱。全球航运业的“准时制”特性意味着这些集装箱现在无法被用于装载其他货物返航，从而造成了进一步的混乱。

**关键材料短缺的影响**
霍尔木兹海峡关闭导致的部分供应短缺，其担忧原因显而易见。石油是石油化学品的必需品，短缺不仅推高了油价，也几乎推高了一切商品的价格。根据牛津能源研究所的数据，中国三分之一的LNG使用量来自该海峡。

尽管某些材料的影响较难量化，但其冲击同样巨大，并且可能对芯片和AI数据中心产业产生不成比例的巨大影响。

LNG通过燃气轮机产生能源，对亚洲（包括中国大陆和台湾地区）的工业供电至关重要。同时，它也被大量用于运行新的AI数据中心，例如xAI和OpenAI运营的那些。CNBC报道称，欧洲LNG价格已上涨超过60%。随着天然气价格上涨，能源价格也将随之攀升，这将使运行这些AI数据中心的成本更加高昂。

氦气对芯片生产极为重要。作为一种化学惰性气体，氦在生产硅片时被用作覆盖气和吹扫气。其高热导率使其在液化后也成为有用的冷却剂。它对芯片制造同样关键。如果短缺持续超过两周，则可能带来重大挑战。

据悉，三星和SK海力士等韩国公司正密切关注局势，因为任何长期中断都可能影响内存供应。这对于本已紧张的行业来说，可能是最糟糕的情况。台湾地区的台积电也可能受到影响，尽管一些台湾公司声称储备了可供数年使用的氦气。

**可预见的持续封锁**
目前没有明确方法可重新开放海峡，甚至没有开始相关努力的时间表，也没有迹象表明相关任何一方愿意启动这一进程。因此，霍尔木兹海峡在可预见的未来很可能将持续关闭。这意味着全球贸易中断将恶化，相关的供应链问题将加剧。

如果你一直在等待内存或其他商品价格回落再行升级，那么你可能需要等待非常长的时间。

items.title: ROG Xbox Ally X gaming handheld to get Auto Super Resolution boost in April — Microsoft touts 30% performance boost thanks to AI-powered image upscaling
items.description: Auto Super Resolution, an OS-level AI upscaling feature for Windows 11, is heading to the ROG Xbox Ally X and could deliver noticeable frame-rate improvements.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The ROG Xbox Ally X gaming handheld will soon be getting a performance upgrade in the form of Auto Super Resolution, or Auto SR. Microsoft confirmed the feature at the ongoing Game Developers Conference (GDC) 2026 and is expected to begin an initial rollout in April 2026. A key benefit of Auto SR is that it operates at the OS level and can run directly on the device using an NPU, in this case, the Ryzen Z2 AI Extreme chip powering the Asus ROG Xbox Ally X. This approach should increase frame rates, reduce load on the GPU, and potentially improve battery life.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCAuto SR was originally introduced as an exclusive feature for Copilot+ PCs with Snapdragon X or Snapdragon X2 processors. It is an AI-powered image upscaling solution designed to work similarly to DLSS, FSR, and other upscaling technologies.According to Microsoft, users can expect a boost in performance by up to 30% when Auto SR is enabled on the handheld console. While that may sound impressive, in a video shared by @ethangach on X, the company showcased a quick demo using the ROG Xbox Ally X, where Forza Horizon 5 ran at 35 FPS on average with no upscaling as compared to 51 FPS average when enabling Auto SR. Click the tweet below to see a video of Microsoft's announcement:Article continues below You may like Microsoft confirms next-gen Xbox, codenamed Project Helix, will be powered by custom AMD SoC and feature 'FSR Diamond' The ROG Xbox Ally handheld console gets its biggest price drop yet, currently $100 off Save $110 on this Asus ROG Xbox Ally handheld, back at a record-low price Xbox announces automatic super resolution mode is coming to the Xbox Ally in April.Leverages AI to scale resolution up in real time to improve performance.Auto SR "fills in the gaps" by existing upscaling tools like DLSS. pic.twitter.com/XDwttHUvxVMarch 12, 2026An important aspect to consider is that enabling Auto SR can introduce some amount of latency, which may or may not affect the gaming experience depending on the title. On top of that, Microsoft’s official documentation says that Auto SR is currently only supported in games that run on DirectX 11 or DirectX 12, although that may change once it adds support for Xbox ROG Ally X.In related news, the company recently confirmed that its next-generation Xbox console, codenamed Project Helix, will be powered by a custom AMD SoC with support for a new upscaling tech called FSR Diamond. Much like Auto SR, FSR Diamond is going to rely on AI-assisted upscaling in addition to ray regeneration for ray tracing and path tracing, as well as multi-frame generation to deliver higher resolutions and improved visual performance. While the next Xbox is still some time away, the introduction of Auto SR on handheld hardware could offer an early glimpse of how Microsoft plans to lean more heavily on AI-driven graphics technologies in the future. items.titleZh: ROG Xbox Ally X游戏掌机4月将支持自动超级分辨率技术——微软宣称借助AI图像增强技术可提升30%性能
items.descriptionZh:
华硕ROG Xbox Ally X游戏掌机即将通过“自动超级分辨率”（Auto SR）功能获得显著的性能提升。微软在2026年游戏开发者大会上确认了此消息，并预计于2026年4月开始初步推送。

**背景与上下文**
Auto SR最初是微软为搭载骁龙X或X2处理器的Copilot+ PC推出的独占功能。它是一种基于人工智能的图像超分辨率技术，其设计目标与英伟达的DLSS、AMD的FSR等超分技术类似，旨在提升渲染效率与画质。此次更新标志着该技术首次从PC平台扩展到手持游戏设备。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
Auto SR的核心创新在于其系统级的集成方式。与需要游戏引擎集成的DLSS或FSR不同，Auto SR在操作系统层面运行，可以直接调用设备上的专用神经网络处理单元来执行AI超分任务。在ROG Xbox Ally X上，其动力源自AMD的Ryzen Z2 Extreme芯片，该芯片集成了强大的NPU。这种架构允许Auto SR在后台自动工作，“填补”传统超分工具的空白，通过智能重构图像细节来提升分辨率。
其关键优势在于减轻了GPU的渲染负担。通过让NPU处理部分画面提升工作，GPU可以更专注于原始帧的渲染或维持更低功耗，从而有望实现帧率提升和电池续航的改善。

**性能参数与对比数据**
根据微软公布的数据，启用Auto SR后，掌机性能预计可提升高达30%。在一段由@ethangach在X平台分享的演示视频中，微软使用ROG Xbox Ally X运行《极限竞速：地平线5》进行了直观对比：
*   **关闭Auto SR时**：游戏平均帧率约为35 FPS。
*   **启用Auto SR后**：平均帧率提升至约51 FPS。
这一表现相当于帧率提升了约45.7%，超出了30%的预期，效果显著。

然而，该技术也存在一定局限性。微软指出，启用Auto SR可能会引入少量的输入延迟，这对于不同游戏的体验影响各异。此外，目前官方文档显示Auto SR仅支持基于DirectX 11或DirectX 12 API的游戏，不过未来可能会针对Xbox ROG Ally X进行适配扩展。

**技术影响与应用场景**
此次更新对于移动游戏场景意义重大。它为掌机玩家提供了在保持视觉画质的同时，显著提升帧率和流畅度的新选择，特别是在硬件资源受限的便携设备上，平衡性能与功耗的需求更为迫切。
从更宏观的战略角度看，在手持硬件上引入Auto SR，揭示了微软未来将更深度依赖AI驱动图形技术的方向。这与其下一代Xbox主机（代号“Project Helix”）的开发战略形成呼应。该主机已确认将采用定制AMD SoC，并支持一项名为“FSR Diamond”的新超分技术。与Auto SR类似，FSR Diamond也将深度融合AI辅助超分、光线重建以及多帧生成等技术，以实现更高的分辨率和视觉保真度。

**总结**
综上所述，ROG Xbox Ally X即将获得的Auto SR升级，不仅是一次直接的性能增强，更是AI技术在消费级游戏图形处理中地位日益核心化的体现。它通过系统级NPU加速，为掌机游戏提供了切实的帧率提升方案，并预示了微软未来在游戏硬件上将持续深化整合专用AI计算单元的发展路径。

items.title: China's ByteDance to access 36,000 Blackwell GPU cluster through Malaysia cloud operator — Nvidia confirms no objections, deal is in line with US export controls
items.description: Nvidia says it is permissible for ByteDance to use AI clusters outside of China to develop its AI prowess as long as these clusters are built in compliance with the U.S. export controls.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Although ByteDance cannot get its hands on Nvidia’s latest Blackwell GPUs to develop its AI products, the company is looking forward to using a cluster containing 36,000 of its B200 GPUs that is physically located in Malaysia, reports the Wall Street Journal. The cluster will be used for research and development purposes as the company wants its share of the global AI pie. Nvidia says it is perfectly legal for the company to use such a cluster as long as it was built in compliance with the U.S. export controls.The cluster, worth around $2.5 billion and consisting of 500 NVL72 GB200 rack-scale systems, will be formally owned and operated by Aolani Cloud in Malaysia. The hardware will be supplied through Aivres, a company that builds servers based on Nvidia GPUs, according to the WSJ, which cites people familiar with the arrangements. An Aolani spokesperson told WSJ that the company currently operates with roughly $100 million worth of hardware, so the scale of the proposed expansion is vast, but it is not completely clear who is funding it. ByteDance is also reportedly considering additional deployments, such as a cluster containing over 7,000 B200 GPUs at a data center in Indonesia.Aolani has been leasing AI servers equipped with Nvidia H100 GPUs in Malaysia to ByteDance since February, 2025, so it is highly likely that the company’s primary focus is to lease AI hardware to the Chinese company. That said, the Hopper-based cluster is likely a test vehicle to ensure that ByteDance can technically use it, and the U.S. regulators do not oppose such usage by granting all the required export licenses. For the planned Blackwell deployment, initial payments have reportedly already been made.Article continues below You may like TikTok owner ByteDance to reportedly purchase $14 billion worth of Nvidia AI GPUs in 2026 Former Chinese gaming company with China govt ties accused of smuggling banned AI GPUs China’s GPU cloud consolidates around Baidu and Huawei as domestic AI chips scale up Aolani was established in late 2023 and operates under a Cayman Islands holding structure, according to company registry documents cited by the WSJ. The company is designated as a Tier-1 cloud partner of Nvidia, which means that it has certification from the developer of AI GPUs and priority access to its newest accelerators, but this does not mean that Nvidia has all the required export licenses from the U.S. government to ship to Aolani. For advanced AI GPUs (H100/B200/B300, etc.), Nvidia must ensure that every shipment complies with U.S. Department of Commerce Bureau of Industry and Security rules.Exports of Nvidia AI accelerators to Malaysia typically do not require a BIS export license, but compliance checks are still mandatory, and licenses may be required in certain cases. To that end, Nvidia’s compliance teams review cloud partners before shipping hardware to them.“All Nvidia cloud partners are evaluated and cleared by Nvidia’s field operations, finance, and compliance teams before they can receive our products, directly or through an OEM,” an Nvidia spokesperson told Tom’s Hardware.While ByteDance is a China-based company, it is not in the Bureau’s Entity List or Military End Use (MEU) list, so its potential use of Nvidia hardware does not automatically trigger a red flag for Nvidia, Aolani, or the U.S. government. Furthermore, the 2023 U.S. export controls primarily regulate where the hardware is shipped, not where its compute oomph is used, which means that the rules were intentionally written to allow global cloud infrastructure based on American hardware. While many U.S. lawmakers are uneasy about usage of American AI accelerators by Chinese companies in the cloud, this is completely legal under the current export rules framework.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors“By design, the export rules allow clouds to be built and operated outside controlled countries,” the Nvidia spokesperson stressed. “Winning the business of those clouds will bring tens of billions of dollars and high paying jobs home. Export controls gave the world’s second largest commercial market to foreign competitors, and America cannot afford to make the same mistake across all of Asia.” Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: 字节跳动将经马来西亚云服务运营商获取3.6万块Blackwell GPU集群——英伟达确认无异议，交易符合美国出口管制规范
items.descriptionZh:
尽管字节跳动无法直接获得英伟达最新的Blackwell架构GPU来开发其AI产品，但据《华尔街日报》报道，该公司正计划使用一个物理位于马来西亚、包含36,000块B200 GPU的庞大计算集群。该集群将用于研发目的，显示了字节跳动意图在全球AI竞赛中占据一席之地的决心。英伟达表示，只要集群的建设符合美国出口管制规定，字节跳动的使用是完全合法的。

这一价值约25亿美元的集群由500套NVL72 GB200机架级系统构成，将由马来西亚的Aolani Cloud公司正式拥有和运营。硬件将通过Aivres（一家基于英伟达GPU构建服务器的公司）供应。一位Aolani发言人表示，公司目前运营着价值约1亿美元的硬件，因此此次规划的扩张规模巨大，但其具体资金来源尚不完全明确。报道还称，字节跳动正考虑在印度尼西亚的数据中心部署另一个包含7,000多块B200 GPU的集群。

自2025年2月以来，Aolani一直在马来西亚向字节跳动出租配备英伟达H100 GPU的AI服务器，因此其业务重点很可能是向这家中国公司租赁AI硬件。现有的基于Hopper架构（H100）的集群很可能是一个技术验证平台，旨在确保字节跳动技术上能够使用，并且美国监管机构通过颁发所有必要的出口许可证不反对此类使用。据报道，针对计划中的Blackwell部署，首期款项已经支付。

Aolani成立于2023年底，采用开曼群岛的控股结构。该公司被指定为英伟达的“一级云合作伙伴”，这意味着它获得了英伟达的认证，并能优先获得其最新加速器，但这并不自动意味着英伟达已获得美国政府向Aolani发货所需的所有出口许可证。对于先进的AI GPU（如H100/B200/B300等），英伟达必须确保每一批货物都符合美国商务部工业与安全局的规定。

向马来西亚出口英伟达AI加速器通常不需要BIS的出口许可证，但仍需进行合规审查，且在特定情况下可能需要许可证。为此，英伟达的合规团队会在发货前对云合作伙伴进行审核。一位英伟达发言人强调，所有英伟达云合作伙伴在直接或通过OEM接收产品前，都必须经过其现场运营、财务和合规团队的评估与批准。

尽管字节跳动是一家中国公司，但它并未被列入BIS的“实体清单”或“军事最终用户”清单，因此其使用英伟达硬件的潜在可能性并不会自动为英伟达、Aolani或美国政府亮起红灯。此外，2023年的美国出口管制主要规管的是硬件运输的目的地，而非其计算能力被使用的地点。这意味着相关规则有意为基于美国硬件的全球云基础设施留下了空间。虽然许多美国立法者对中国公司在云端使用美国AI加速器感到不安，但这在当前的出口规则框架下是完全合法的。

英伟达发言人强调：“出口规则的设计本就允许在受控国家之外构建和运营云服务。赢得这些云服务的业务将为美国带回数百亿美元的资金和高薪工作岗位。出口管制已将全球第二大商业市场让给了外国竞争对手，美国不能再在整个亚洲重蹈覆辙。”

items.titleZh: 购买Ryzen 7 9850X3D处理器与高端X870E主板，即可免费获得价值399美元的海盗船复仇者内存——新蛋捆绑套装为可超频AM5平台核心组件节省436美元。
items.descriptionZh:
近日，Newegg电商平台推出了一款极具吸引力的高端PC硬件组合优惠，将AMD顶级的游戏处理器、华硕旗舰超频主板以及高速DDR5内存打包出售，为追求极致性能的用户提供了一个高性价比的装机选择。

该组合的核心部件是AMD的Ryzen 7 9850X3D处理器，这款8核16线程的Zen5架构处理器被视为当前市场上最快的游戏CPU。其基础频率为4.7 GHz，最大加速频率可达5.6 GHz，相比前代9800X3D有显著提升。最关键的是，它采用了AMD的3D V-Cache技术，拥有高达96MB的三级缓存，这对提升游戏帧率和响应速度有极大助益。同时，其120W的热设计功耗（TDP）也使其在提供顶级性能的同时，相对更容易进行散热管理。

与处理器搭配的是华硕ROG Crosshair X870E Apex主板，这是一款专为极限超频设计的高端产品。其采用了加强型的供电设计，并配备了双内存插槽（最高支持128GB），这种配置旨在最大限度地减少信号干扰，为用户手动压榨DDR5内存和CPU性能提供了最佳平台。对于不熟悉手动超频的用户，华硕也提供了一系列自动超频辅助技术。除了超频特性，该主板还拥有丰富的扩展性：包括后置10个USB接口（其中两个为40Gbps的USB4）、Wi-Fi 7和5GbE有线网络、五个M.2插槽（其中两个支持PCIe 5.0）以及新一代音频解决方案。该主板单独零售价高达725.99美元，是AM5平台当之无愧的旗舰之选。

组合中的内存是海盗船复仇者系列32GB（2x16GB）DDR5-6400 CL36 RGB套条。其6400MHz的频率正好落在AMD Zen5平台的性能甜点区间，黑色的散热片配以贯穿式的磨砂RGB灯条，外观颇具辨识度（灯光可通过主板控制关闭）。更重要的是，该内存采用了海力士芯片，与华硕这款Apex主板搭配具备优秀的超频潜力。这套内存单独购买需要399.99美元。

本次促销活动的最大亮点在于其惊人的价格优势。这套包含三大件的组合包原价总计为1,624.95美元，而Newegg的捆绑售价仅为1,187.99美元，直降436.99美元。这意味着，在购买顶级处理器和主板的同时，这套32GB的高性能DDR5内存几乎是免费赠送的。此外，Newegg还为该组合增加了额外赠品：一个华硕TUF Gaming M4 Air轻量化游戏鼠标和一份《赤色沙漠》游戏（价值约125.99美元），使得整体性价比进一步凸显。

总而言之，这款组合套装精准定位了高端游戏玩家和硬件发烧友。它提供了一个近乎“一步到位”的AM5平台顶级解决方案，用户无需再为处理器、主板和内存的兼容性与性能搭配费心。对于计划组建一台用于高分辨率、高刷新率游戏，并希望兼顾一定超频乐趣的高性能PC用户而言，此次限时捆绑优惠无疑是一个绝佳的入手时机。

items.title: How to protect yourself from bad external SSDs during the PC hardware apocalypse – newer drives will definitely cost more, and some may offer up shockingly poor performance
items.description: If you’re shopping for a new external SSD, you might want to buy a drive soon, and something that was released before the AI demand seemingly gobbled up all the good flash.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The voracious hardware demands on AI hyperscalers have been driving up prices (and driving down availability) of all kinds of PC hardware for half a year now. If you’re after a new external SSD, prices have so far nearly doubled from their all-time lows. But compared to the price of RAM, an RTX 5090, or an internal SSD, spending 70-80% more for external storage than you would have paid this time last year is a relative bargain. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)And if you do need a new external drive, you might want to buy one soon. Because new drive launches in 2026 seem to be few and far between, as we should expect given the current demands on the supply of flash storage. And it seems likely that the few companies that are launching new external drives may be having to make do with some… less-than-ideal NAND. Exhibit A: Take a look at the sustained writes from this Orico drive I recently reviewed.In our real-world file transfer test, it was at least in the range of other drives of its class.Article continues below You may like Kioxia exec says the AI boom means the era of the cheap 1TB SSD is over Best External SSDs 2026 SSDs now cost 16x more than HDDs due to AI supply chain crisis (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)But when it comes to sustained write speeds, the results were the worst I’ve seen for any SSD since I started testing external storage for Tom’s Hardware. We use Iometer to hammer the SSD with sequential writes for 15 minutes to measure the size of the write cache and performance after the cache is saturated. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Keep in mind that we tested a 512GB review sample, so the faster SLC cache will be larger on higher-capacity models. But if and when you do enough fast writing to hit the native NAND speed, you are looking at writes in the 60-80 MB/s range – with occasional dips below 50 MB/s.This performance was so low that I reached out to Orico to make sure this was the expected speed and we weren’t dealing with a faulty drive. A representative told me that, while higher-capacity models would have more SLC cache and perform slightly better overall, “the product is functioning normally and there are no quality issues.”In short, the drive was working as expected, while delivering sustained write speeds that are worse than hard drive write speeds, and even worse than many of the best flash drives we’ve tested. I had to get a closer look.Opening the BookDrive (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Prying the BookDrive open, the controller for the internal M.2 drive is a DRAMless Maxiotek map1202C, which Google tells me has been used in various drives going back as far as 2019. I couldn’t identify the maker of the flash, though. Neither the two alphanumeric strings silkscreened on the NAND packages showed up in search results, and there doesn’t seem to be another label underneath what’s on the surface of the chips.But regardless of who made them, the flash in this “new” external SSD has the slowest write performance of any solid-state drive I’ve tested in at least a decade – and possibly ever. Even Intel’s 2008-era X25-M internal SSD managed to hit 72.2 MB/s writes in our testing — I didn’t start testing SSDs until sometime in 2009.External storage in 2026Granted, the surprisingly bad performance of one recent drive may not definitively tell us a whole lot about all the external storage drives launching in 2026. But we’re already in mid-March, and Sandisk is the only major SSD company I’m aware of that has announced new portable drives this year. We’re awaiting those SSDs for testing, and I fully expect them to perform much better than the Orico drive above. What to read next Micro Center lists 8TB WD_Black SSD at $2,419 MSRP, real price tag up 50% since October Sandisk crushes wallets with up to 2.8X SSD price hikes as NAND shortage strangles the market Hard drive prices have surged by an average of 46% since September — iconic 24TB Seagate BarraCuda now $500 as AI claims another victim But Sandisk’s new mid-range (20 Gbps) Extreme V3 drive sells for $459.99 direct from SanDisk (supposedly marked down from $574.99). And given the 20 Gbps interface bandwidth cap, how much better could that new drive be than Crucial’s X10 Pro drive that I tested (and was generally impressed by) back in 2023? Parent company Micron may have publicly ditched its consumer-focused Crucial brand in favor of the lucrative AI market late last year, but the X10 Pro is still readily available, and at a current $259 for the 2TB model, it’s priced $200 (just shy of 44%) less than Sandisk’s brand-new drive at the same capacity.It seems pretty safe to assume that any new external SSDs from big-name brands will be priced similarly to Sandisk’s recently announced drives. Because, from a business standpoint, any stock that flash companies are holding back from their eager AI customers to put into consumer products is probably a financial risk. And any new product from smaller drive makers (generally those who aren’t making their own flash), if there are many new products at all, will have to make do with whatever scrap flash is available that both AI companies and the flash makers themselves don’t want. Chances are, both the performance and the endurance of the flash in those drives will be questionable at best.Buy soon, and consider an older SSD (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)In short, if you need a new solid-state external drive, I would advise looking at something that came out more than six months ago (before the rise of AI hyperscaler demand), that has been thoroughly tested (by Tom’s Hardware or some other site you trust), and that is still available at a reasonable price. Our Best External SSD page has six great solid-state options that we’ve tested.Because who knows how long the stock of those older drives is going to last? While some new drives this year may be a little faster, they’re almost certainly going to be significantly more expensive than what’s available now. Any new drives from smaller companies may fail to meet even basic SSD performance expectations from several years ago. If you wait long enough and the market doesn’t improve, you may be stuck buying a drive that both costs much more and performs much worse than what’s available now.
items.author: Matt Safford
items.titleZh: PC硬件危机下如何避开劣质外置固态硬盘——新硬盘必然涨价，部分性能或差得惊人
items.descriptionZh:
人工智能（AI）超大规模计算中心对硬件的巨大需求，近半年来持续推高所有个人电脑硬件的价格并降低其可用性。如果你正在寻找新的外置固态硬盘（SSD），其价格已从历史低点近乎翻倍。然而，与内存、RTX 5090显卡或内置SSD的价格涨幅相比，外置存储设备比一年前同期贵出70-80%反而显得“相对划算”。

如果你确实需要一个新的外置硬盘，可能得尽快购买。因为鉴于目前对闪存供应链的需求压力，2026年新款硬盘的发布预计将寥寥无几。并且，少数发布新外置硬盘的公司，可能将不得不使用一些“不尽如人意”的NAND闪存。

**典型案例：性能堪忧的新品**

以近期评测的一款Orico BookDrive外置SSD为例。在实际文件传输测试中，其表现尚在其同类产品范围内。但在持续写入速度方面，其结果是笔者为Tom's Hardware测试外置存储以来所见过的最差成绩。

测试使用Iometer工具进行15分钟持续顺序写入，以测量写入缓存大小及缓存饱和后的性能。评测样品为512GB容量，更高容量的型号其更快的SLC缓存区会更大。然而，一旦进行足够多的高速写入以至于触及NAND闪存的原生速度，其写入速度将降至60-80 MB/s的范围，甚至偶尔会跌破50 MB/s。

这一性能过低，以至于笔者联系Orico以确认这是预期速度而非故障产品。对方代表确认，虽然更高容量型号会有更大SLC缓存且整体性能稍好，但“产品功能正常，不存在质量问题”。简而言之，该硬盘工作状态符合预期，但其持续写入速度却低于机械硬盘的写入速度，甚至逊于许多测试过的优质U盘。

**拆解探寻根本原因**

拆开BookDrive后发现，其内部的M.2 SSD采用了无DRAM缓存的Maxiotek MAP1202C主控（可追溯至2019年）。闪存芯片的制造商则无法识别，芯片表面的两串字符在搜索中无结果。无论制造商是谁，这款“新”外置SSD中的闪存，其写入性能是笔者至少十年来（甚至可能是有史以来）测试过的所有固态硬盘中最慢的。作为对比，英特尔2008年发布的X25-M内部SSD在测试中都能达到72.2 MB/s的写入速度。

**2026年外置存储市场展望**

诚然，单一新款硬盘的糟糕表现未必能代表2026年所有新品。但截至三月中旬，已知只有闪迪（Sandisk）等极少数主流SSD公司宣布了新的便携式硬盘。我们期待测试这些新品，并预计其性能将远优于上述Orico产品。

然而，闪迪新款中端（20 Gbps接口）Extreme V3驱动器在官网售价为459.99美元（标注原价574.99美元）。考虑到20 Gbps的接口带宽限制，它又能比2023年测试时给人留下深刻印象的Crucial X10 Pro（2TB型号现价259美元）好多少呢？后者比闪迪同容量新品便宜200美元（约44%）。

可以合理推测，其他知名品牌的新款外置SSD定价将与闪迪新品类似。因为从商业角度看，闪存公司从急切的人工智能客户手中预留库存用于消费级产品，可能是一种财务风险。而规模较小的驱动器制造商（通常不自产闪存）的任何新产品，将不得不依赖于人工智能公司和闪存制造商自身都不想要的“边角料”闪存。这些硬盘中闪存的性能和耐用性很可能最多只能说是“存疑”。

**购买建议：趁早入手，考虑旧型号**

总之，如果你需要一个新的外置固态硬盘，建议考虑购买半年前（AI超大规模需求激增之前）发布的、经过充分测试（如Tom's Hardware或其他可信网站）、且目前价格仍合理的产品。

因为没有人知道这些旧型号硬盘的库存还能维持多久。虽然今年的一些新驱动器可能会快一点，但它们几乎肯定会比现有产品贵得多。而来自小公司的新品，其性能甚至可能达不到几年前对SSD的基本预期。如果等待时间过长且市场未见改善，你最终可能被迫购买一个比现有产品价格高得多、性能却差得多的驱动器。

items.title: Silicon Power US RMA policy now hedges against AI-driven RAM and SSD shortages — company says it will refund the original purchase price 'if there is a shortage of replacement products'
items.description: Most RAM and SSD manufacturers still offer in-warranty replacements as Silicon Power U.S. hedges policy against shortages
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter RAM shortages are prompting some unheard-of moves on the part of some companies, like Transcend telling its customers that all deliveries were delayed, and Phison's CEO warning that smaller consumer electronics players might be headed for closure. Now, Silicon Power (SP) has added a notification to the header of its online U.S. store stating that "a full refund of the original purchase price will be issued if there is a shortage of replacement products." The new measure hints that, in some cases, SP is unable to source replacement parts or that the cost of replacement parts is now so high as to be unfeasible.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI shortages (Image credit: Nvidia)AI data centers are swallowing the world's memory and storage supplyChip scarcity assaults auto industry amid the worsening Nexperia and DRAM crisisSamsung and SK hynix shorten memory contracts as pricing power shifts back to suppliersMemory makers are set to earn $551 billion from the AI boomThe latter is highlighted by the recent adventures of an Australian enthusiast's struggle to return his RAM kit for a warranty replacement, owing to the fact that the kit has quadrupled in price since he bought it. Retailers try their best to protect themselves from scarce availability and constantly fluctuating prices, all while consumers have to resort to paying highly inflated prices for what were once affordable staple items.SP's statement is conditional and seems to indicate that in the case of an RMA, the SP store will make an attempt at replacing the item, but also prepares buyers for the possibility that they won't get a replacement unit. This is almost certainly due to the fact that flash chips have multiplied in price and become absurdly scarce, particularly for smaller companies.Article continues below You may like Memory vendor under fire for imposing hefty 15% depreciation fee on returns despite skyrocketing RAM value Retailer denies memory replacement due to 4x increase in DDR5 pricing, says price increase would equate to an 'upgrade' for the customer 'This memory situation is a multi-year problem,' says Maingear CEO As for its UK web-store, Silicon Power doesn't have a specific notice, and its warranty policy simply states that "due to ongoing technology or in case of discontinuity of any product, SP will be unable to repair the product, and therefore replace the product with a comparable one."We took the opportunity to do a cursory browse of major memory and SSD manufacturers' RMA policies, and at the present time, we found that at least on paper, almost every major player says it will honor a replacement. Having said that, as the crises tighten, these policies might well change, and sooner rather than later. Note that our findings are consumer advice and not legal analysis:Corsair - Replacement: "If your Corsair product is not operating in accordance with its published technical specifications, the product will be replaced at Corsair’s expense."G.Skill - Replacement, conditional: "Any replacements provided will be with the same product model. This warranty obligation is conditioned upon the hardware being returned to the original place of purchase, or another place as directed by G.SKILL [...]."Kingston - Replacement: "Kingston will, at its option, either repair or replace any part of its products that prove defective by reason of improper workmanship or materials."Western Digital - Replacement, then equivalent item, then refund: "Western Digital will provide a Customer Replacement Unit (CRU) [...] it may instead provide a product with at least equivalent capabilities to the failed Product. [...] If Western Digital cannot provide a CRU replacement or an equivalent product, it may instead provide the user with a credit."Samsung - Replacement, then refund at current market value: "(1) repair or replace the Product with new or refurbished Product of equal or greater capacity and functionality; or (2) refund the then current market value of the Product at the time the warranty claim is made to SAMSUNG."Sandisk - Company chooses replacement or refund at current market value. "(1) repair or replace the Product with a new reconditioned or refurbished Product of equal or greater capacity, or another equivalent product; or (2) refund the current market value of the Product at the time the warranty claim is made."The fact that nearly every company says it will honor a replacement is relevant against the backdrop of regional consumer protection laws. Most buyers' primary point of contact will be the store where they bought their hardware, but while most regional laws provide the buyer with multiple degrees of protection, for primary Western markets, the seller is apparently not obligated to replace the item and can instead issue a refund if said replacement would prove too onerous.That seems to be the case with the European Union directive 2019/771, article 13.1.2.a, that sates that the consumer does have a choice between repair or replacement, "unless the remedy chosen would be impossible or, compared to the other remedy, would impose costs on the seller that would be disproportionate". The RAM price rise of 4-5x since last year would likely qualify, though individual EU member-states may offer additional protection, however.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe situation doesn't look too different in the United Kingdom, where the Consumer Rights Act of 2015 says, in section 23, item (3)(a), that although the customer has a choice between replacement or refund, they also "cannot require the trader to repair or replace the goods if that remedy (the repair or the replacement) [...] (b) is disproportionate compared to the other of those remedies."As for the United States, most major PC hardware makers offer limited warranties, as opposed to full warranties, generally meaning that the manufacturer's policy dictates available options, save when specific state or regional laws apply. Broadly speaking, the stores are merely intermediaries in the process, and any protections they offer are a bonus.The land down under has what appear to be the most consumer-oriented laws, as recently highlighted by the aforementioned Umart case. A browse of the Australian government's page dedicated to returns, refunds, and replacements clearly states that in case of a major failure of the item, "the consumer can choose," and should the seller be unable or unwilling to replace the item, the customer can "ask for a replacement [or] ask for a refund".What this all means is that, should a person's RAM or SSD break under the manufacturer's warranty period in most regions, it might be wiser to try and go directly to the manufacturer instead of the place of sale, particularly considering that RAM almost always carries a limited lifetime warranty.Just don't be too surprised if the AI component crisis continues to impact returns and refund policies, as other well-known manufacturers might be forced to follow SP, due to the shortage of DRAM or NAND flash chips. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Bruno Ferreira
items.titleZh: 硅侠宣布新保修政策规避AI硬件缺货风险——承诺"若更换品缺货"将按购买价退款
items.descriptionZh:
当前，全球正面临一场严峻的内存与存储供应链危机，其核心驱动力源于人工智能（AI）数据中心建设的爆炸式需求。AI服务器对高带宽内存（如HBM）和大容量存储的巨额消耗，正在吞噬本已紧张的DRAM和NAND闪存产能，导致供应严重向企业级市场倾斜，进而挤压了消费级产品的可用资源。这场短缺不仅造成了价格的飙升，更引发了一系列前所未有的连锁反应，直接冲击了终端消费者的售后权益保障。

供应链危机的直接表现是内存条和固态硬盘（SSD）关键芯片的价格在过去一年内暴涨了4至5倍，且现货极其稀缺。这种市场状况迫使零售渠道和制造商重新评估其传统的保修与更换政策。创新点或说行业的新动向在于，一些公司开始引入前所未有的免责条款，以应对可能无法履行更换义务的风险。例如，十铨科技（Silicon Power）已在其美国官网的显著位置添加通知，声明若无法获取替换产品，将直接按原始购买价格进行全额退款。这一举措实质上揭示了行业现状：对于许多厂商，尤其是规模较小的公司，获取用于售后换新的闪存芯片已变得不可能或成本高到难以承受。

此次危机的深度和广度通过多方面数据得到印证。一方面，有消费者案例显示，因其欲返修的内存套件当前市价已是购买时的四倍，零售商以“相当于为用户免费升级”为由拒绝了换新请求。另一方面，主要内存制造商的财务预期也反映了这一趋势，他们预计将从AI热潮中获得巨额收入，这进一步加剧了消费级市场的资源挤占。在保修政策的具体执行层面，尽管各大厂商的书面条款目前仍承诺提供换新服务，但措辞中已暗含条件与变数。例如，三星和闪迪的条款写明，可更换产品或按提出保修申请时的“当前市场价值”退款；西部数据则设置了“提供等效产品”或“提供信用额度”的后续选项。这些条款在内存价格飞涨的背景下，为厂商提供了灵活处理的空间，可能使消费者无法获得同型号产品的直接替换。

从技术影响和应用场景来看，这场危机的影响是全方位且深远的。在消费端，它意味着消费者长期以来依赖的“终身保修”或“换新”承诺正在变得脆弱。用户可能面临的情况是：产品损坏后，要么获得一笔远低于当前重置成本的退款，要么被迫接受不同型号或品牌的“等效”产品，升级体验受阻。在行业端，危机加速了市场洗牌，资源向三星、SK海力士等头部存储巨头以及拥有强大采购权的顶级品牌（如金士顿、海盗船）集中，而中小品牌和渠道商的生存空间被严重压缩，甚至有倒闭风险。其影响已从个人电脑升级、游戏主机扩展，蔓延至汽车电子等依赖稳定芯片供应的行业。

不同地区的消费者保护法律在此次危机中扮演了关键角色，但提供的防护力度不一。欧盟和英国的法律虽然原则上赋予消费者选择维修或替换的权利，但均包含“如果该补救措施不可能或给卖家造成不成比例的成本”则可豁免的条款。当前内存价格数倍的增长，很可能被卖家援引为“不成比例的成本”从而拒绝换新。美国的情况更依赖于制造商自身的有限保修条款，零售商通常仅作为中间渠道。相比之下，澳大利亚的法律对消费者最为有利，明确规定对于存在重大缺陷的商品，消费者有权选择更换或退款，卖家不能单方面以成本过高为由拒绝。

综上所述，本次由AI需求激化引发的存储供应链危机，已从上游制造环节渗透至下游消费权益领域，改变了行业惯例。对于消费者而言，在产品保修期内出现故障时，直接联系制造商而非零售商可能是更有效的途径。然而，随着DRAM和NAND闪存短缺的持续，预计将有更多制造商被迫效仿十铨科技的做法，调整其保修政策，消费者需要对此有充分的心理预期。这场危机预计将持续多年，成为重塑存储市场格局与消费体验的关键变量。

history.title: Tech titans team up to form optical interconnect alliance to solve the AI buildout's big data bottleneck — Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom & more set sights on building PHY to break through the limitations of copper
history.description: AMD, Nvidia, Microsoft, Broadcom, and Meta have formed an Optical Compute Interconnect Multi-Source Agreement (OCI MSA) to develop a standardized optical interconnect for AI data centers, with the aspiration to build a PHY capable of handling speeds of up to 3.2 Tb/s.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter This week, AMD, Broadcom, Nvidia, OpenAI, Meta, and Microsoft announced plans to standardize a protocol-agnostic, scale-up interconnect for AI data centers. The Optical Compute Interconnect Multi-Source Agreement (OCI MSA) group is tasked with defining an open connectivity specification for optical interconnections in AI data centers. This would allow for higher domain scale-up sizes, and enable a multi-vendor supply chain for optical interconnects, which the ongoing AI infrastructure buildout demands.The group's primary goal is to enable data centers to scale by using optical interconnections rather than relying solely on copper, which is currently hitting its physical limits for optimal data transfer speeds and power consumption. Copper is also facing significant supply chain constraints, and an industry-wide shift to optical interconnections would alleviate some of this demand. An optical interconnection would also bolster data transfer speeds, crucial for large-scale AI workloads.For copper, pushing electrical signals to high speeds results in signal degradation and unsustainable levels of power consumption; the solution is optical interconnects. Copper is inherently a lossy, resistant medium, necessitating huge amounts of power to send data over distances at high speeds. An optical physical layer (PHY) can overcome this electrical resistance challenge, allowing for higher-speed data transmission. The goal of the newly-founded group is to develop a PHY capable of delivering up to 3.2Tb/s and beyond.Article continues below You may like AMD, Broadcom, and Nvidia join hyperscalers to define optical scale-up interconnect of the future for AI clusters Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneck Start-up plans to use terahertz radio frequencies for communication between servers instead of copper or optical connections Given that power is an ongoing challenge in AI data center buildouts, a solution that not only stabilizes power usage but also increases interconnection speeds seems like an obvious choice.Optical cables would let more systems be concurrently connected over greater distances, without many of the penalties that come with copper, improving scale-up domains. However, optics also comes with its own downsides: Failure rates, increased heat output, higher overall costs, and overall failure rates. With the technology nascent in its application, new standards must be created in order for it to mature.Going platform-agnostic (Image credit: Intel)"Optical cables and the silicon photonics technology already exist when it comes to connecting different switches as part of a pluggable transfer ecosystem.” said Vivek Raghunathan, CEO and co-founder at Xscape Photonics, in a recent interview with Tom's Hardware Premium. Indeed, TSMC's COUPE technology is a foundational bedrock for enabling optical and photonics in chips; where the new OCI MSA standard comes in is to enable these physical chips to effectively travel across the same lanes.The open standard developed by the OCI MSA would allow multiple vendors within the optical supply chain to offer components to a singlular, unified spec. In theory, it should drive down the cost of optics and optical interconnects at scale. Moreover, it divests the sole reliance on TSMC, as the standard would ensure interoperability between products and chips made with COUPE, and those using alternative CPO packaging platforms, effectively de-risking optical supply chains in the process.If co-packaged optics were introduced into data centers without an effective rulebook or guidelines to govern them, each vendor design would become proprietary, siloed between one another. So for larger-scale data centers that run multiple ecosystems, the OCI MSA's new spec defines a standard to let them drive on the same roads and use the same data pathways. This isn't about hooking together systems and getting them to understand each other via NVLink or UALink, but instead about offering a physical foundation to allow those protocols to travel over fibre connections.Within a data center, the OCI MSA's newly defined standard would allow a data center to theoretically run NVLink for Nvidia chips and UALink for AMD hardware, while making use of the same underlying optical infrastructure, at higher speeds than copper can realistically allow.“Fundamentally, the current copper-based interconnects just cannot meet that bandwidth requirement," Raghunathan continued. The OCI MSA's standard supports a range of optical solutions, such as pluggable optical modules, on-board optics, and chips using co-packaged optics. This would effectively break the barriers that copper currently employs. What to read next Nvidia invests $4 billion into photonics firms in a bid to bolster data center interconnect supply chains UALink roadmap plots course to optimized AI data center interconnects Bill Gates-backed silicon photonics startup develops optical transistors 10,000x smaller than current tech Getting in on the ground (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The companies involved in the newly formed alliance are all of the usual suspects: Nvidia, AMD, Microsoft, OpenAI, Broadcom, and Meta all create their own AI accelerators in some way, shape or form. Letting them assist in defining the new PHY means their products will be supported by it from day one."Scale up focused optical technologies, protocols, and switch architectures are foundational to building scalable, multi rack, high performance AI compute domains. The OCI MSA advances this vision with a forward-looking physical layer specification setting the stage for open standards, differentiated implementations and systems architecture innovation.” said Saurabh Dighe, Corporate VP of Azure and Architecture at Microsoft.From an early deployment at 200Gbps speeds all the way up to the 3.2Tb/s outlined by the alliance, it's becoming increasingly clear that to serve larger frontier models, or build toward vastly more complex AI data centers, optics is no longer an exploratory zone. It must be rolled out and served directly, if hyperscalers and AI model developers have their way."By equipping best-in-class compute with state-of-the-art optics, the OCI MSA can deliver the scale and performance required by the next era of super-intelligence." says Gilad Shainer, SVP of networking at Nvidia. With superintelligence on the mind, speed is a non-negotiable, and the standards being drawn up reflect the rapid advancement of data center technology.
history.author: Sayem Ahmed
history.titleZh: 科技巨头联手组建光学互连联盟，破解AI基建的大数据瓶颈——英伟达、AMD和博通等公司着力开发物理层技术以突破铜缆限制。
history.descriptionZh:
本周，AMD、博通、英伟达、OpenAI、Meta和微软共同宣布成立光学计算互连多源协议组织，旨在为人工智能数据中心定义一个协议无关的、可扩展的光互连开放标准。这一举措旨在应对当前AI基础设施扩建中铜互连所面临的物理极限、功耗挑战和供应链约束，通过推动光学互连的标准化，构建一个多供应商的开放生态系统，从而支持更大规模、更高性能的AI计算集群。

当前数据中心主要依赖铜缆进行设备间互连，但在追求极高数据传输速率时，电信号在铜介质中会产生严重的衰减和失真，导致功耗急剧上升且传输距离受限。光学互连利用光子而非电子传输数据，其物理层能从根本上克服电阻损耗，实现更高速、更长距离且能效更高的数据传输。新成立的OCI MSA组织的核心目标是制定一个物理层规范，支持高达3.2Tb/s及以上的传输速率，为AI数据中心的横向扩展提供基础。

该标准的关键创新点在于其“协议无关”和“供应商中立”的特性。它并非定义新的通信协议，而是为现有的互连协议提供一个统一的光学物理层基础。这意味着数据中心可以在同一套光互连基础设施上，同时运行英伟达的NVLink、AMD的UALink或其他专有互连协议，从而实现异构硬件环境的高效整合。此外，标准将涵盖可插拔光模块、板载光学以及共封装光学等多种形态，提供灵活的部署选项。

在性能方面，光学互连有望突破铜缆在带宽和能效上的天花板。铜缆在高速长距离传输时面临严峻挑战，而光学方案能够稳定支持从200Gbps直至3.2Tb/s的速率，同时显著降低单位比特的传输功耗。这对于训练万亿参数级别的前沿AI模型至关重要，因为此类任务需要海量计算节点间持续进行低延迟、高带宽的数据交换。

此举对产业链将产生深远影响。首先，一个开放的标准化接口将打破当前光学供应链可能存在的技术壁垒和供应商锁定，鼓励更多厂商参与竞争，从而有望降低光学互连的整体成本。其次，它降低了对单一制造技术的依赖。虽然台积电的COUPE等硅光子集成技术是重要的使能基础，但该标准将确保采用不同共封装光学平台的产品之间能够互操作，增强了供应链的弹性。

从应用场景看，该标准直接服务于构建大规模、多机柜的AI计算域。随着AI模型规模不断膨胀，计算集群需要跨越数百甚至数千个加速器，节点间的互连带宽和延迟成为系统性能的关键瓶颈。光学互连是实现这种“横向扩展”的理想选择，它能以更低的功耗连接更多系统，覆盖更远距离，从而支持未来更复杂的超级智能计算基础设施。

各大科技巨头联合推动此事，反映了行业共识：光学互连已从探索性技术转变为AI数据中心发展的必然路径。微软Azure架构副总裁指出，面向扩展的光学技术、协议和交换架构是构建可扩展高性能AI计算域的基础。英伟达高管也强调，为顶尖计算配备尖端光学技术是实现下一代超智能所需规模与性能的关键。随着标准的制定与落地，光学互连将为核心AI基础设施建设开启新的篇章。

history.title: Nvidia claims 1 million times better path tracing performance is coming in future gaming GPUs — says current GPUs are already 10,000x faster than Pascal
history.description: At GDC 2026, Nvidia held a presentation somehow aimed at gamers and not data center clients. Still, it was sprinkled with AI pat-on-the-backs, with the company touting that its future gaming GPUs will offer 1,000,000 better path tracing performance. And the current-gen Blackwell family is apparently already 100,000x better due to dedicated Tensor and RT cores.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Despite increasing competition from Intel and AMD, Nvidia's RTX lineup remains the best hardware for ray tracing and path tracing in games. Ever since Turing, RTX 20 series, the company has made significant strides — mostly leveraging AI and neural rendering — to increase graphical fidelity without compromising performance. Now, at GDC 2026, it's claiming that the future holds an even more impressive milestone. Driving Innovation and RTX Advances with John Spitzer, VP of Developer and Performance Technology - YouTube Watch On During the presentation, John Spitzer (Dev & Performance VP) presented a line graph that plotted the progress of ray tracing and path tracing performance in Nvidia's gaming GPUs. At the far-left corner, we see Pascal, aka the legendary RTX 10 series, which came out a decade ago. Comparing that to today's Blackwell GPUs (RTX 50), the path tracing performance has apparently improved by 10,000 times already.That's largely due to a focus on hardware-accelerated neural rendering enabled by dedicated RT and Tensor cores that handle machine learning inside Nvidia GPUs. Features like DLSS are entirely reliant on AI; the ability to piece together frame data more accurately in both upscaling and frame-gen situations is only possible due to models trained on Nvidia's supercomputers.Article continues below You may like Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says "the future is neural rendering" at CES 2026 Moore Threads unveils next-gen gaming GPU with 15x performance and 50x ray tracing improvement Nvidia introduces DLSS 4.5 and Multi Frame Generation 6X at CES 2026 Spitzer says that Moore's Law is dead and that silicon advancements alone wouldn't be enough to generate photorealistic visuals in his lifetime. Nvidia wants to achieve a level of graphical fidelity that's indistinguishable from real life, but that would require a "hundred or thousand times more computational power" — this is where AI becomes the catalyst. (Image credit: Nvidia)In the future, AI advances will take gaming GPUs to 1,000,000 times better path tracing performance when compared to the RTX 10 series. Newer, faster, more efficient hardware blocks will basically make neural rendering the default going forward, as already claimed by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang. Games would "look like a film" while still running smoothly due to multiple frames being interpolated in real-time by AI.None of this is a revelation — of course, things are supposed to get better over time — but the wait might not be too long. The next-gen Rubin GPUs from Nvidia, slated to launch sometime between 2027 and 2028, could usher in this 1-million-times better path tracing reality. The list of games supporting path tracing is already growing at a rapid pace, with Resident Evil Requiem being the latest addition.As such, the presentation also included some bits about new path tracing technologies, such as ReSTIR (recent spatiotemporal resampling algorithms) and RTX Mega Geometry. To showcase this, Nvidia brought a tech demo for Witcher 4 with over two trillion triangles in the scene, depicting realistic foliage and lighting simultaneously. Make sure to check out the video linked above for more details.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 英伟达声称未来游戏显卡光追性能将提升百万倍——表示现有显卡已比帕斯卡架构快万倍
history.descriptionZh:
在2026年游戏开发者大会（GDC）上，英伟达开发者与性能技术副总裁约翰·斯皮策展示了公司GPU在光线追踪与路径追踪性能方面的巨大飞跃。尽管面临英特尔和AMD的竞争加剧，英伟达的RTX系列目前仍是游戏光线追踪和路径追踪的最佳硬件选择。

自图灵架构（RTX 20系列）以来，英伟达主要借助人工智能和神经渲染技术，在不牺牲性能的前提下显著提升了图形保真度。斯皮策展示的性能进展曲线图显示，与十年前的帕斯卡架构（RTX 10系列）相比，当前布莱克架构（RTX 50系列）的路径追踪性能已提升约**1万倍**。这一成就主要归功于专注于硬件加速的神经渲染，该技术由英伟达GPU中专用的RT核心和Tensor核心驱动，用于处理机器学习任务。例如，DLSS（深度学习超级采样）完全依赖于AI，其能够在超分辨率和帧生成场景中更精确地重构帧数据，正是基于英伟达超级计算机训练的模型。

斯皮策指出，摩尔定律已失效，仅靠硅基工艺进步不足以在其有生之年实现照片级真实感的视觉效果。英伟达的目标是实现与现实难以区分的图形保真度，但这需要“数百或数千倍的计算能力”，而AI正是实现这一目标的催化剂。英伟达CEO黄仁勋也曾表示“未来是神经渲染”。未来，AI的进步将使游戏GPU的路径追踪性能达到RTX 10系列的**100万倍**。更新、更快、更高效的硬件模块将使神经渲染成为未来的默认技术。由于AI实时插值生成多帧，游戏将能够“看起来像电影”同时保持流畅运行。

这一未来可能并不遥远。英伟达下一代鲁宾架构GPU预计在2027年至2028年间推出，可能将带来这百万倍性能提升的现实。目前支持路径追踪的游戏列表正在迅速增长，最新加入的是《生化危机：安魂曲》。

演示中还介绍了一些新的路径追踪技术，如ReSTIR（近期时空重采样算法）和RTX Mega Geometry。为展示后者，英伟达带来了《巫师4》的技术演示，场景中包含超过**两万亿个三角形**，同时描绘了逼真的植被和光照效果。这一进展标志着，通过硬件与AI的深度融合，实时渲染正在向影视级视觉体验迈进。

history.title: The ongoing Strait of Hormuz blockage will impact the semiconductor and AI industries with Aluminum, Helium and LNG shortages — and with no timeline for re-opening, supply chains face significant challenges
history.description: As global oil prices spike due to the ongoing pseudo-blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, other shortages are looming, and they could hit the chip and AI industries dramatically. Aluminum and helium for chip production have been impacted, but shortages of LNG could also cause problems for gas turbines powering many AI data centers.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.
